body:
The multi-colored Christmas lights strung across the ceiling, lighting you in a multitude of colors you as we sat on my bed, you somehow missing the way my eyes taking in every inch of you in your cute PJs. You laughed at some video on your phone, leaning across me to show my once you finish it, your head right beside mine, I watch the silly video, as I discreetly smell the strawberry shampoo in your hair, savoring the softness of your skin where your shoulder touches min. You face is literally inches away inches away from my own, yet I dare not touch, despite the images in my head.

So, “Who do you have a crush on?” you follow up, noticing my disinterest in the video. “I don’t wanna talk about it,” I trying to not stare at your shape, I look to the wall, where our shadows overlap, elongated and tangled by the fairy lights, and me imagining us tangled together in much more fun ways.

You continue to lean on me, scrolling through your phone, totally oblivious to the way the warmth of your skin burns through me straight to my heart. “You’re weirdly quiet tonight,” you mention, feeling the vibration of your voice echo through me w
reblog_comment:
The multi-colored Christmas lights strung across the ceiling, lighting you in a multitude of colors you as we sat on my bed, you somehow missing the way my eyes taking in every inch of you in your cute PJs. You laughed at some video on your phone, leaning across me to show my once you finish it, your head right beside mine, I watch the silly video, as I discreetly smell the strawberry shampoo in your hair, savoring the softness of your skin where your shoulder touches min. You face is literally inches away inches away from my own, yet I dare not touch, despite the images in my head.

So, “Who do you have a crush on?” you follow up, noticing my disinterest in the video. “I don’t wanna talk about it,” I trying to not stare at your shape, I look to the wall, where our shadows overlap, elongated and tangled by the fairy lights, and me imagining us tangled together in much more fun ways.

You continue to lean on me, scrolling through your phone, totally oblivious to the way the warmth of your skin burns through me straight to my heart. “You’re weirdly quiet tonight,” you mention, feeling the vibration of your voice echo through me where your head rests on my shoulder. I close my eyes for a moment, imagining your fingers in my hair and lips against my own, instead of you just leaning against me. But instead I reply, “I don’t even wanna think about it.”

You decide to improve my mood by being silly, flopping on your back, head in my lap, “Okiedokie, I know you’ll tell me when you’re ready! Cuz we’re friends forever, right?” You grin up at me and wrap your arms around my waist, you give me a huge hug to help, burying your face in my stomach in the process, and I bite my lip, desperately trying to hide my reaction, with your face so close to down there that it gives me so many thoughts of something better, but knowing you have no clue, despite the fire it’s stoking inside me, somehow, I just end up feeling alone…

But at least I got you in my head, oh yeah
At least I got you in my head, in my head
Sleepovers in my bed, oh yeah
At least I got you in my head, in my head
In my head
In my head…
